title: Cubase Project Migration
summary: Repair a Cubase project that opens with missing plugins by substituting equivalent installed VSTs, then save the migrated session, export stems, and capture a verification screenshot.
taskPrompt:
Goal: Migrate a Cubase project to this computer by replacing all missing/invalid VST plugins with equivalent ones from the available plugins, then export stems and a mixdown.

Software:
- Cubase: \software\Cubase.lnk

Input:
- Cubase project: \input\project.cpr

Before you begin:
1. Generate the list of VST3 plugins installed on this VM and save it to `\output\available_vsts.txt`. In PowerShell:
   `$roots = @('C:\Program Files\Common Files\VST3','C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\VST3'); $items = @(); foreach ($r in $roots) { if (Test-Path $r) { $items += Get-ChildItem -Path $r -Recurse -Filter *.vst3 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name } }; ($items | Sort-Object -Unique) | Out-File -FilePath '\output\available_vsts.txt' -Encoding ascii`
2. Open the Cubase project at `\input\project.cpr` using the Cubase shortcut at `\software\Cubase.lnk` (e.g. double-click the .lnk, or `Start-Process '\software\Cubase.lnk'`).

Steps:
1. When the "Missing Plugins" dialog appears, note all unavailable VST plugins.
2. For each track with a missing VST instrument or effect:
   a. Open the track's instrument or insert slot.
   b. Replace the missing VST with a functionally equivalent one from the available plugins listed in `\output\available_vsts.txt`.
   c. Choose a preset or patch that best approximates the original sound character (e.g., replace a missing string library with an available string patch).
3. Play back the project and verify all tracks produce audible, artifact-free audio.
4. Export stems: for each track/channel as organized in the project (do NOT split or reorganize tracks — export exactly as the project defines them), solo it and export audio mixdown (WAV, 44.1 kHz or higher) to \output\stems\<track_name>.wav
5. Save the migrated project to \output\migrated_project.cpr
6. Take a screenshot of the Cubase MixConsole or arrange view showing all tracks with their replaced plugins visible:
   save_milestone_screenshot(path="\output\overview.png", description="Cubase project with replaced plugins")

Output files (all saved to \output):
- migrated_project.cpr: Cubase project with all missing VSTs replaced.
- stems/: Per-track solo WAV exports (one per track).
- overview.png: Screenshot of MixConsole or arrange view.
agentMustDo: Replace each missing or invalid VST with a functionally equivalent installed plugin from `input/available_vsts.txt`.
Verify the migrated project plays back with audible, artifact-free tracks.
Export one WAV stem per project-defined track or channel to `output/stems/`.

inputFiles.name: Cubase project
inputFiles.description: The broken Cubase session that opens with missing plugins on the target VM.

inputFiles.name: Available VST list
inputFiles.description: Agent-visible list of installed VST plugins that can be used as replacements.

referenceFiles.name: Migrated Cubase project
referenceFiles.description: Saved Cubase project with missing plugins replaced.

referenceFiles.name: Exported stems
referenceFiles.description: One WAV export per project-defined track or channel.

referenceFiles.name: Overview screenshot
referenceFiles.description: Cubase screenshot showing the migrated project with tracks and plugin assignments visible.

evaluation:
**Hard gate conditions:** `output/migrated_project.cpr` must exist; `output/stems/` must contain at least one non-silent WAV stem; and `output/overview.png` must pass a Cubase screenshot gate.

**Weighted scoring when all gates pass:** stem completeness and quality contributes `0.20` (`valid matched stems / expected reference stems`), and timbral similarity contributes `0.80` (mean MFCC-based cosine-similarity score, with `>= 0.70` receiving full credit).

**What does a PERFECT output look like?** A migrated Cubase project, a full set of non-silent unclipped stems matching the reference track inventory, and a valid Cubase screenshot, for a score near `1.0`.

**What does a WRONG or empty output look like?** Missing required files, silent stems, or a non-Cubase screenshot cause an immediate `0.0`; poor plugin substitutions keep the gate passing but reduce the timbral score.

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env

classifier.rationale: The task involves audio project migration and VST plugin management, which fits within audio and music production.
